task_completion.description: Open CurrencyConfig.java in the IntelliJ IDEA editor.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The editor tab shows 'CurrencyConfig.java' is open and active in IntelliJ IDEA.

task_completion.description: The currency symbols display correctly, indicating successful reload in Windows-1252.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The editor content shows the Euro (€), Pound (£), and Yen (¥) symbols clearly, without any replacement symbols (?) or garbage characters.

task_completion.description: The file is converted to UTF-8 encoding.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The file encoding indicator in the bottom right status bar of IntelliJ IDEA displays 'UTF-8'.

task_completion.description: The line separators are changed to Unix (LF).
task_completion.visual_evidence: The line separator indicator in the bottom right status bar of IntelliJ IDEA displays 'LF'.

task_completion.description: Project Encoding settings are configured to UTF-8.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Settings/Preferences window is open to 'Editor > File Encodings', showing 'Project Encoding' set to 'UTF-8'.

task_completion.description: CurrencyTest unit tests are run and pass successfully.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Run tool window at the bottom shows 'CurrencyTest' executed with green checkmarks indicating all tests passed.

integrity.description: The agent used the IntelliJ IDEA GUI to perform the encoding and line separator changes.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show interaction with IntelliJ menus, status bar popups, and settings windows, rather than terminal commands.

integrity.description: The tests were run using IntelliJ's built-in test runner.
integrity.visual_evidence: The test results appear in the standard IntelliJ Run/Debug tool window with the graphical test tree, not just as text output in a terminal.

integrity.description: The agent did not use terminal commands to bypass the IDE configuration.
integrity.visual_evidence: There should be no terminal windows showing commands like 'iconv' or 'dos2unix' used to modify the files directly.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The target file is 'CurrencyConfig.java'. The expected currency symbols to look for in the code are Euro (€), Pound (£), and Yen (¥). The final expected file encoding is UTF-8, and the line separator must be LF. The unit test file to run is 'CurrencyTest.java'.
